title: "A Wounded Deer Leaps Highest,"
content:
A wounded deer leaps highest,
    I've heard the hunter tell;
    'T is but the ecstasy of death,
    And then the brake is still.

    The smitten rock that gushes,
    The trampled steel that springs;
    A cheek is always redder
    Just where the hectic stings!

    Mirth is the mail of anguish,
    In which it cautions arm,
    Lest anybody spy the blood
    And "You're hurt" exclaim!
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in ""A Wounded Deer Leaps Highest,""...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
